LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ALLISON AIELLO'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE TO SANTA CLARA IS DENIED. DEFENDANT AIELLO SHOWS THAT SHE RESIDES IN SANTA CRUZ COUNTY, BUT DEFENDANT FAILS TO MEET HER BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT SAN FRANCISCO VENUE IS IMPROPER. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE INJURY DID NOT OCCUR IN SAN FRANCISCO. UNDER CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE 395, SAN FRANCISCO IS A PROPER VENUE BECAUSE THE COE DECLARATION FILED IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION INDICATES THAT SOME OF THE DISCLOSURES AND INVASIONS OF PRIVACY OCCURRED IN SAN FRANCISCO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DISCOVERY 302, NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MARY HUGHES, HUGHES FINANCIAL ENTERPRISES, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PROPOUNDED ON PLAINTIFF AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 13, 2013 TO GIVE DEFENDANTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANTS MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. KATHERINE HUIBONHOA, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR APR-09-2013 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 10:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S) OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST DEFENDANT(S) ALLISON AIELLO, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS NETOUT. THE FEB-20-2013 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. DEFENDANTS MARY HUGHES AND HUGHES FINANCIAL ENTERPRISES, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PROPOUNDED ON PLAINTIFF AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE ESSENCE OF PLAINTIFFS' SPECIFIC OBJECTIONS TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS THAT THE SERVERS HAVE BEEN TAMPERED WITH. WHILE THAT MAY BE TRUE, THAT DOES NOT RENDER RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS NON-DISCOVERABLE. WHETHER OR NOT THOSE DOCUMENTS ARE ADMISSIBLE AT TRIAL IS AN ENTIRELY DIFFERENT QUESTION, WHICH IS NOT BEFORE THIS COURT. IF THERE ARE PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS, THEY MUST BE SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITH RESPECT TO DOCUMENT REQUEST 14, WHICH SEEKS THE PRODUCTION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IN A RELATED CASE. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENT IS LIKELY TO BE RELEVANT IN SUPPORTING EITHER PARTY'S CASE. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE DENIED AS MOOT. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER ANY OBJECTIONABLE EVIDENCE IN ARRIVING AT THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SERVE AMENDED RESPONSES TO 5-8, 10-13, 15-27, 28-36, 38 AND TO PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHIN TEN DAYS. SANCTIONS AGAINST GABRIEL JACKSON AND HER ATTORNEYS ARE ORDERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,500. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PROTEM JUDGE: TOM COHEN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Jun-17-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CONSTANCE F. MORRISON OF JACKSON JENKINS & RENSTROM LLP,
TEL. NO. 982-3600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALLISON AIELLO'S Motion For Change Of Venue to Santa Clara is denied. Defendant Aiello shows that she resides in Santa Cruz County, but Defendant fails to meet her burden of establishing that San Francisco venue is improper. Defendant fails to show that the injury did not occur in San Francisco. Under Civil Procedure Code 395, San Francisco is a proper venue because the Coe Declaration filed in opposition to the motion indicates that some of the disclosures and invasions of privacy occurred in San Francisco. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Prodcution Of Documents Propounded On Pltf And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: TODD THACKER, ESQ., 415.982.3600, FOR PLAINTIFF GABRIEL JACKSON.
KIRK FREEMAN, ESQ., 415.398.1082, FOR DEFENDANT MARY HUGHES.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. DEFENDANTS MARY HUGHES and HUGHES FINANCIAL ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For ProduCtion Of Documents Propounded On PlAINTIFF And For Sanctions IS Granted in part and denied in part. The essence of Plaintiffs' specific objections to production of documents is that the servers have been tampered with. While that may be true, that does not render responsive documents non-discoverable. Whether or not those documents are admissible at trial is an entirely different question, which is not before this Court. If there are privileged documents, they must be specifically identified in a privilege log. The motion is denied with respect to Document Request 14, which seeks the production of the settlement agreement in a related case. It does not appear that this confidential document is likely to be relevant in supporting either party's case. The Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The objections to evidence are denied as moot. The Court did not consider any objectionable evidence in arriving at this order. Plaintiff is ordered to serve AMENDED RESPONSES TO 5-8, 10-13, 15-27, 28-36, 38 AND to produce responsive documents within ten days. Sanctions against Gabriel Jackson and her attorneys are ordered in the amount of $3,500. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Protem Judge: Tom Cohen, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.
